content:
We launched Zalanx today — security monitoring for lean SaaS teams.

Most SaaS teams already use several tools across cloud, identity, payments, observability, app monitoring, and edge security.

Tools like:

• Cloudflare

• Clerk

• Auth0

• Supabase

• Stripe

• AWS

• GCP

• Azure

• Sentry

• Datadog

• Okta

• Vercel

But suspicious activity is often scattered across too many dashboards.

A suspicious login may appear in one tool.

A suspicious IP may appear in another.

A cloud event may be somewhere else.

An application alert may be in another dashboard.

Zalanx gives SaaS teams one lightweight workspace to monitor suspicious activity, review incidents, understand related activity, use Timeline and Graph views, download reports, and evaluate safe policy-controlled response actions.

It is built for SaaS founders, CTOs, DevOps teams, agencies, MSPs, and cloud/security consultants that need better visibility before they have a full security operations team.

Current features include:

• Overview dashboard

• Incidents

• Integrations

• Threat Intelligence

• Defense Actions

• Timeline

• Graph

• Reports

• Workspace security posture

• PDF and JSON report downloads

We are also exploring:

• Custom webhook/API ingestion for internal tools

• Postmortem-ready incident timeline exports

• Markdown incident summaries

• Better workflows for small SaaS teams reviewing security events

The goal is not to replace your existing cloud, auth, payment, or observability tools.

The goal is to help small teams review security-relevant activity from one place before they are ready for complex enterprise security operations.

Product:

https://app.zalanx.com/

We would love feedback from developers, DevOps engineers, SaaS founders, and security/cloud people.

Especially around:

• Is the positioning clear?

• Would custom webhook/API ingestion be useful?

• Are Timeline and Graph views useful for incident review?

• What would make this more useful for small SaaS teams?
